title: Method for Determining Fajr Time
subtitle: Fajr begins at Subh Sadiq (true dawn) and lasts until sunrise
body:
There is no disagreement among scholars regarding the start and end of the Fajr time. It begins at Subh Sadiq and remains until the sun starts to rise.

Subh Sadiq occurs when the sun is 18 degrees below the horizon. In countries like Bangladesh, India, Pakistan, Afghanistan, and parts of America and Europe, Subh Sadiq is calculated at 18 degrees. This value may vary for other countries. When you select your country, this degree is automatically set, which you can view in the settings under "Prayer time calculation".

Fajr time ends the moment the sun begins to rise. Based on your selected country or GPS location, the app calculates the exact sunrise time for that specific area. To be certain, you can cross-check our sunrise timing with Google or reliable websites like timeanddate.com. Please note there might be a 1-2 minute difference compared to locally printed calendars.

title: Method for Determining Zuhr Time
subtitle: From the moment the sun declines westward until the start of Asr
body:
Zuhr time begins when the sun passes the meridian (the highest point in the sky) and starts declining toward the west. There is no scholarly dispute regarding the start time. However, regarding when Zuhr time ends, there are two prominent opinions based on Hadith.

The first opinion is that Zuhr lasts until the shadow of an object becomes equal to its length. The second opinion states that Zuhr remains until the shadow of an object becomes twice its length.

Imam Abu Hanifa (Rh.) acted upon the second opinion and issued rulings regarding the end of Zuhr based on it. On the other hand, Imam Shafi'i (Rh.), Imam Malik (Rh.), Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal (Rh.), and the two famous students of Imam Abu Hanifa—Imam Yusuf (Rh.) and Imam Muhammad (Rh.)—acted upon and issued rulings based on the first opinion. Essentially, all major Imams except Imam Abu Hanifa (Rh.) agree on the earlier end time for Zuhr.

title: Method for Determining Asr Time
subtitle: Asr starts immediately after Zuhr and lasts until Maghrib begins
body:
Asr time begins as soon as Zuhr time ends, and it concludes when the sun starts to set. There is no disagreement among scholars on this general rule.

When does Asr start?

Based on Hadith, there are two famous opinions regarding the end of Zuhr and the start of Asr.

Shadow Length 1x (Shafi'i, Maliki, Hanbali)

According to this view, Zuhr ends and Asr begins when the shadow of an object equals its length. Following this, Zuhr ends relatively earlier, and Asr starts earlier.

This is the position of the Shafi'i, Maliki, and Hanbali schools of thought. Additionally, Imam Abu Hanifa’s two prominent students, Imam Yusuf and Imam Muhammad (Rh.), issued rulings based on this. This method is commonly followed in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Malaysia, and many other parts of the world.

Shadow Length 2x (Hanafi)

The alternative view is that Zuhr remains until the shadow of an object becomes twice its length. Asr begins only after the shadow reaches this double length. Consequently, Zuhr lasts longer, and Asr starts later.

This is the Hanafi position. It is the standard practice in countries like Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan.

The App supports both methods

Visit "Asr Calculation Method" in the app settings. You can select either option or choose "Automatic," which will display the time based on the prevailing practice of your current country.

For example, while in Bangladesh, it will show timings consistent with local Masjids. If you travel to Saudi Arabia, it will automatically adjust to the timing followed in their Masjids.

When does Asr end? Can I perform Salat during sunset?

Performing Salat during sunset is prohibited. However, if someone has not yet performed that day's Asr due to an unavoidable reason, they may perform only that specific Asr even while the sun is setting. No other Nafl or Qaza Salat can be performed during this time. For this reason, the app shows the sunset period as part of Asr time, while also marking it as a prohibited time.

Nevertheless, delaying it this much is highly discouraged. Doing so intentionally without a valid excuse is Makruh (disliked) and a cause for sin.

title: Method for Determining Maghrib Time
subtitle: Maghrib starts at sunset and lasts until Isha
body:
Maghrib begins the moment the sun is fully submerged. Regarding the end time, there are two opinions. According to Imam Abu Hanifa (Rh.), after sunset, a red glow appears in the sky, followed by a white glow. Maghrib remains until this white light disappears. According to Imam Shafi'i (Rh.) and the Two Disciples (Imam Yusuf and Muhammad), Maghrib ends as soon as the red glow disappears.

Since the duration between the red and white glow is short, no sectarian distinction is made regarding the end of Maghrib in this app. Like other international platforms such as islamicfinder.org, we maintain a unified end time for Maghrib.

It is worth noting a common misconception that "Maghrib only lasts 15-20 minutes." This is incorrect. Depending on the season, Maghrib can last around 1 hour and 15 minutes. However, it goes without saying that Salat should be performed at the start of the time, and delaying it without reason is improper.

title: Why is Maghrib time showing so long?
subtitle: Isha starts immediately after Maghrib time ends
body:
Many believe Maghrib time is extremely short, often thought to be no more than 15-20 minutes. Consequently, if someone is delayed by 20 minutes, they might skip it, thinking the time has passed and they should just pray it as Qaza with Isha.

This is a misconception.

Depending on the season, Maghrib can last between 1 hour 15 minutes to 1.5 hours. While delaying it is Makruh, it is still valid. Therefore, you should perform Salat as soon as it begins.

Our app displays the full duration of the time. This is accurate and verified by Islamic scholars, ensuring that if you are delayed, you can still perform your Salat within the valid window rather than missing it due to a lack of information.

title: Method for Determining Isha Time
subtitle: From the end of Maghrib until the start of Fajr
body:
Isha time begins as soon as Maghrib ends and concludes at the start of Fajr (Subh Sadiq). This is the consensus among the Imams of the four major schools of thought.

Ruling on Isha after Midnight

Performing Isha after midnight is Makruh (disliked) but permissible. This means if performed after midnight, it is still considered "Ada" (on time) rather than "Qaza." However, doing so without a valid excuse is a sinful act.

To learn more about the duration of Isha with evidence, you can read our blog post here: https://muslimsday.com/blog/end-time-of-salatul-isha

title: Notes on Tahajjud and Post-Maghrib Nafl
body:
Tahajjud

The best time for Tahajjud is during the last third of the night. This is a highly virtuous time, and during Ramadan, waking up for Sahari provides a golden opportunity to perform it.

Narrated by Abu Hurayrah (Ra): The Messenger of Allah (pbuh) said:

Our Lord, the Blessed, the Superior, comes every night down to the nearest Heaven of the world when the last third of the night remains, saying: "Is there anyone to invoke Me, so that I may respond to his invocation? Is there anyone to ask Me, so that I may grant him his request? Is there anyone seeking My forgiveness, so that I may forgive him?" (Bukhari 1145)

If someone doubts they can wake up for the latter part of the night, they may perform it before sleeping to still receive the reward, InshaAllah. There is no fixed number of Rakats, though the Prophet (pbuh) usually performed 8. There are no specific Surahs required for this Salat.

It is recommended to recite the Qur'an as long as possible. If you do not know long Surahs, you can repeat what you know. For example, if you know 10 small Surahs, you can recite 5 in each Rakat. One should also prolong the Ruku and Sujud, and utilize the prostration for making various Duas.

Nafl Salat after Maghrib

The Prophet (pbuh) used to perform Nafl Salat between Maghrib and Isha. While no Sahih Hadith specifies a fixed number of Rakats, you may perform as many as you wish.

Descriptions of 6 or 20 Rakats exist in narrations, though their chains (Isnad) are weak (Da'if). However, many companions (Sahaba), followers (Tabi'un), and later scholars practiced Nafl Salat during this window. This indicates that this practice is highly recommended even if specific number-related narrations are weak.

There are no special rules or specific Surahs for this prayer. It is performed in sets of 2 Rakats just like other Nafl prayers.

Anas (Ra) said, "The companions used to stay awake and perform Nafl Salat between Maghrib and Isha." (Abu Dawud, 1323, 1324)

The Tabi'i Hasan al-Basri (Rh) used to say that Salat between Maghrib and Isha is also considered part of the Night Prayer or Tahajjud. (Abu Dawud, 1323)

This prayer is commonly known as Awwabin in our region. While Sahih Hadiths refer to Salat al-Duha as Salat al-Awwabin, this post-Maghrib prayer can also be called Awwabin. Firstly, because "Awwabin" refers to those who turn to Allah frequently, and secondly, because various Mursal Hadiths and Athar (reports) use this title for it. Imam Shawkani (Rh) mentioned in Nayl al-Awtar that there is no contradiction in both prayers being called Awwabin. [Kifayat al-Mughtazi, 4/63]

title: Prohibited Times for Salat
subtitle: It is forbidden to perform Salat during sunrise, sunset, and at high noon
body:
There are three specific times when performing Salat is Haraam (forbidden) in Islamic Shariah.

The three times are:

(1) During Sunrise: From the moment the sun begins to rise until it is fully above the horizon. Most scholars estimate this as approximately 10 minutes, while some suggest 15 minutes for caution.

(2) At High Noon: When the sun is at its zenith (directly overhead). This lasts for about 5 minutes.

(3) During Sunset: When the sun turns pale or red before setting until it is completely submerged. This takes about 15 minutes.

Note: If a person has not performed the Asr of that specific day, they may do so even while the sun is setting. However, no other Salat is permitted during these times.

Note: These durations may vary slightly based on geographical location and season.
